prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a very dark, low-visibility shot. The scene appears to show an outdoor setting with shadowy building-like shapes or structures in the background, but the details are difficult to distinguish.

By around 10 seconds, the image changes to a blue-toned, layered composition. A blond, child-like figure or toy is shown in profile facing right, with one arm bent as if running or moving forward. On the left side there is a darker silhouetted figure, also appearing human-like. In the background on the right is a dark silhouette resembling the Statue of Liberty. The lower part of the frame contains dark grass or foliage, giving the scene an outdoor foreground.

The final frame becomes a clear product shot. A Duracell battery is centered horizontally against a pale blue background. The battery has a copper-colored top section on the left and a black body with the word “DURACELL” in white letters. The Statue of Liberty silhouette remains in the background on the right, and grass is visible along the bottom edge. Large white text at the bottom reads: “You Can’t Top The Copper Top.”
